item.title: 通过 nginx 代理 websocket | Proxying websockets via nginx
item.description:
做我自己。 有一个适用于 HTTP 的现有 nginx 配置设置，但现在大家伙要求我也通过它隧道 websocket 连接。

在 nginx conf 的http部分中，添加这些

http { ... map $http_upgrade $connection_upgrade { default upgrade; '' close; } ... }

在 nginx conf 的http > server > location部分中，添加

http { ... server { ... location ... { ... proxy_set_header Upgrade $http_upgrade; proxy_set_header Connection $connection_upgrade; ... } } }

戳nginx

一切正常！

做我自己。 回去看电视。



原文:

Be me. Have an existing nginx config setup that works for HTTP, but now big guy ask me to also tunnel websocket connections through it.

In the nginx conf, in the http section, add these

http {
    ...

    map $http_upgrade $connection_upgrade {
        default upgrade;
        '' close;
    }

    ...
}

In the nginx conf, in the http > server > location section, add

http {
    ...

    server {
        ...

        location ... {
            ...

            proxy_set_header Upgrade $http_upgrade;
            proxy_set_header Connection $connection_upgrade;

            ...
        }
    }
}

Poke nginx

Everything works!

Be me. Go back to watching TV.
item.author: enteio

item.title: 在不那么特殊的 NFT 合约中使用 Chainlink VRF 版本 2 | Using Chainlink VRF version 2 in a not-so-special NFT contract
item.description:
如标题所述，我们将在智能合约中使用 Chainlink VRF 的第 2 版，并了解如何使用它在智能合约中添加可验证的随机性。 在此期间，我们将创建一个 NFT 合约，然后将其部署到 Polygon 的孟买测试网。 作为顶部的樱桃，我们将看到如何从 OpenSea 的智能合约中列出铸造的 NFT。 我们的 NFT 图像没有这样的界限，可以从您的个人资料图片到稀有口袋妖怪或神秘的恶魔果实模型，但我们不会专注于整个“附加 metadata.json”部分，因为这超出了本文的范围文章。

本文假设您熟悉 Alchemy 并拥有一个 Mumbai Testnet RPC URL 用于相同或等效的内容。 此外，如果您想遵循验证部分，您需要有一个 Polygonscan API 密钥。 因此，出于这些原因，请在开始之前随意浏览有关这些主题的资源。

有很多东西要讲，但字太少。 所以，让我们继续吧。

正如许多读者可能知道的那样，在 VRF 之前，要走的路是使用块哈希和/或时间戳或这些行上的东西来引入伪随机性。

这是一个很好的第一步。 但它有一个巨大的明显缺陷。 可以预见。 正因为如此，许多智能合约被利用——尤其是使用 NFT 甚至投注的区块链早期游戏。 由于人们可以有效地计时他们的调用，他们最终可能会获得更高的财富。 这不仅不公平，还对整个生态系统造成了影响，因为 Web 3 - 一些本应在没有信任的情况下工作并且“不可利用”（至少在早期是这样推销的）的东西现在不再如此了。

当然，还有其他竞争者，比如 Provable（后来更名为 Oracalize），但 Chainlink VRF 比它们都更持久，并成为引入链外“可验证”（因此得名可验证随机函数）随机性的首选。

在版本 1 中，智能合约只能从预言机查询 1 个随机数 ( uint256 )。 这在第二个版本中有所改变，智能合约可以查询多个并获得一组uint256数字。

一次可查询的随机数上限由特定链上的 VRF Coo​​rdinator Contract 设置。 例如，对于 Rinkeby、孟买和其他测试网上的 VRF 协调员合约，在撰写本文时上限为 500。

您可能认为这为开发人员提供了很大的回旋余地。 但正如 Buterin 叔叔曾经开发的那样：

在实践中，这可能会有所不同。 虽然在像 Polygon 这样的 L2 或像 BSC 和 Avax 这样的侧链和分叉上可能并不重要，但在我看来，这是在像 Eth 1.0 这样的气体受限环境中展示责任的好方法。

此外，与版本 1 不同，Chainlink VRF v2 引入了订阅管理器门户。 基本上，您在此处创建订阅并向其添加资金。 您会从中获得一个订阅 ID，您需要在您的智能合约中使用它，我们将在下面看到。 然后添加消费者合约（将使用 Chainlink VRF 的合约地址），仅此而已。

老实说，整个流程让我想起了使用 Chainlink Oracle 服务查询需要setFulfillmentPermission()的 API。

创建订阅

在我们编写智能合约之前，我们需要在我们想要访问 Chainlink VRF 的特定链上创建一个订阅。 前往 VRF 订阅管理器门户并单击创建订阅。 这将创建一个导致创建订阅的事务。 您需要在下一步中添加资金并使用 LINK 代币为您的订阅提供资金。

一旦该交易确认，它将提示添加消费者。 这是我们现在停下来的地方。 您现在应该有一个订阅 ID。 这将由智能合约使用，因此请务必记录下来。

写合同

// SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT pragma solidity ^0.8.4; import "@chainlink/contracts/src/v0.8/interfaces/VRFCoordinatorV2Interface.sol"; import "@chainlink/contracts/src/v0.8/VRFConsumerBaseV2.sol"; import "@openzeppelin/contracts/token/ERC721/ERC721.sol"; import "@openzeppelin/contracts/token/ERC721/extensions/ERC721URIStorage.sol"; import "@openzeppelin/contracts/utils/Counters.sol"; contract OurNFTContract is ERC721, ERC721URIStorage, VRFConsumerBaseV2 { using Counters for Counters.Counter; VRFCoordinatorV2Interface COORDINATOR; uint64 subscriptionId; address vrfCoordinator = 0x7a1BaC17Ccc5b313516C5E16fb24f7659aA5ebed; bytes32 keyHash = 0x4b09e658ed251bcafeebbc69400383d49f344ace09b9576fe248bb02c003fe9f; uint32 callbackGasLimit = 200000; uint16 requestConfirmations = 3; uint32 numWords = 2; struct NFTStat { bool exists; address originalOwner; address currentOwner; string characterName; uint8 power; uint8 specialPower; uint16 cooldown; uint256 rarity; } mapping(uint256 => string) requestToCharacterName; mapping(uint256 => address) requestToSender; Counters.Counter private _tokenIdCounter; mapping(uint256 => NFTStat) private characterRegistry; event ReceivedRandomness( uint256 reqId, uint256 n1, uint256 n2); event RequestedRandomness( uint256 reqId, address invoker, string name); constructor(uint64 _subscriptionId) ERC721("OurNFTContract", "ONC") VRFConsumerBaseV2(vrfCoordinator) { COORDINATOR = VRFCoordinatorV2Interface(vrfCoordinator); subscriptionId = _subscriptionId; } ... }

合同不会很复杂。 它的扭曲将在铸币部分。 我们将使用 Openzeppelin 的 ERC721 合约以及包和 Chainlink 合约中的其他所需合约。 在此示例中，我们将继承ERC721 、 ERC721URIStorage （用于存储相应 NFT 元数据 JSON 的 URI）和VRFConsumerBaseV2 （回溯到需要如何继承ChainlinkClient以使用 Oracle Contract 查询任何 API）。

在构造函数后面的行中，我们定义了一些重要的变量。

  1. 在这里， COORDINATOR用于与 Mumbai Testnet 上的 Coordinator 合约进行交互。
  2. 其中大部分可以通过构造函数进行初始化，以在合约中提供更动态的方法，但我们在这里将其初始化为固定值。 接下来的三个可以根据需要进行修改，并将分别指定履行功能的气体限制、协调者必须等待的确认数和要发回的字数。
  3. 由于 NFT 将在 OpenSea 上上市，它的一个直接功能就是能够出售它。 如果是这样，那么 NFTStat 是跟踪存在、当前和原始所有者、角色名称、力量、特殊力量和冷却统计数据的便捷工具。 铸造不会是瞬间的。 当用户通过提供角色名称调用safeMint()函数时，该函数将向 Coordinator 合约发送请求。 协调器合约将通过fulfillRandomWords()方法发回请求的随机词。 由于我们无法访问提供的角色名称和用户地址（ msg.sender不会在履行函数中工作），我们将它们映射到发送给协调器的请求 ID，然后在内部访问它完成fulfillRandomWords()函数。
  4. 一旦fulfillRandomWords()完成执行，它将发出使用ReceivedRandomness事件在特定请求 ID 上接收到的随机词（数字在这里称为词）。

构造函数接收我们从订阅管理器获得的订阅 ID，并通过将vrfCoordinator地址传递给VRFCoordinatorV2Interface来初始化协调器。 我们的初始部分到此结束。

接下来，在下面的safeMint()函数中，我们传入角色的名称。 这是我们 NFT 的属性之一。 这也可以帮助我们跟踪来自协调器合约的请求。

 contract OurNFTContract is ERC721, ERC721URIStorage, VRFConsumerBaseV2 { ... // Assumes the subscription is funded sufficiently. function safeMint(string calldata name) public returns(uint256 requestId) { // Will revert if subscription is not set and funded. requestId = COORDINATOR.requestRandomWords( keyHash, subscriptionId, requestConfirmations, callbackGasLimit, numWords ); requestToCharacterName[requestId] = name; requestToSender[requestId] = msg.sender; emit RequestedRandomness(requestId, msg.sender, name); } function fulfillRandomWords( uint256 requestId, uint256[] memory randomWords ) internal override { uint256 w1 = randomWords[0]; uint256 w2 = randomWords[1]; uint8 p = uint8(w2 % 10); uint8 sp = uint8(w2 % 100 /10); uint16 c = uint16(w2 % 1000 / 100); address sender = requestToSender[requestId]; string memory name = requestToCharacterName[requestId]; uint256 tokenId = _tokenIdCounter.current(); _tokenIdCounter.increment(); _safeMint(sender, tokenId); characterRegistry[tokenId] = NFTStat( true, sender, sender, name, p, sp, c, w1 % 10000 ); emit ReceivedRandomness(requestId, w1, w2); } ... }

一些读者可能注意到上面的一个区别是requestID从bytes32到uint256的变化。 这是 v2 的细微差别之一，在我看来是一个微妙但很好的步骤。 我们在上述函数中从COORDINATOR接收requestId ，然后使用它来映射requestToCharacterName和requestToSender映射中提供的字符名称和铸币者地址。 最后，该函数发出一个RequestedRandomness事件。

上面的fulfillRandomness()函数由 Coordinator 调用并接收requestId和randomWords数组。 前一个参数与我们通过调用safeMint()获得的请求 ID 相同，后者包含我们请求的 2 个随机数。

这里需要注意的是，虽然您可以在任何合约方法中通过 VRF 向 Coordinator 发送请求以获取随机数，但具有当前函数原型的fulfillRandomness方法是必须的。 否则，你会从编译器收到“你需要将你的合约转换为抽象”的通知。 这是因为我们继承的VRFConsumerBaseV2使它成为强制性的。 只有当从 VRF 服务发回结果时，协调器才会调用此函数。

这就是为什么我们在这里进行 NFT 的实际铸币。 我们取出传回的两个随机数，然后从第二个随机数中计算出威力、特殊威力和冷却时间。 第一个随机数的最后 4 位用作稀有度。

您可能知道，在可靠性中使用小数并不是我们所拥有的奢侈品，因此最后 4 位将转换为小数点右侧有两位数的数字。 我们取回铸币者的地址和提供的角色名称。

然后，我们增加tokenIdCounter ，然后将带有tokenId的 NFT 铸造到该地址。 最后，我们发出ReceivedRandomness事件，该事件向我们展示了我们从 VRF 接收到的针对特定requestId的两个uint256随机数。

下面的getCharacter()函数是一个实用函数，它为我们提供了铸造 NFT 的元数据。 这将由元数据服务器使用。

 contract OurNFTContract is ERC721, ERC721URIStorage, VRFConsumerBaseV2 { ... function getCharacter(uint256 tokenId) public view returns ( address, address, string memory, uint8, uint8, uint16 ) { require(characterRegistry[tokenId].exists == true, "Character does not Exist."); return ( characterRegistry[tokenId].currentOwner, characterRegistry[tokenId].originalOwner, characterRegistry[tokenId].characterName, characterRegistry[tokenId].power, characterRegistry[tokenId].specialPower, characterRegistry[tokenId].cooldown ); } ... }

那不是全部。 还记得我们想在 NFT 易手时跟踪它的统计数据吗？ 可以肯定的是，任何体面的智能合约都会调用safeTransferFrom() ，但一些开发人员可能不想通过在他们的市场中实现IERC721Receiver接口来添加onERC721Received()的额外保护（请参阅我的 NFT 市场文章） . 因此，我们还需要重写transferFrom()函数，如下所示。

 contract OurNFTContract is ERC721, ERC721URIStorage, VRFConsumerBaseV2 { ... function transferFrom( address from, address to, uint256 tokenId ) public virtual override { super.transferFrom(from, to, tokenId); characterRegistry[tokenId].currentOwner = to; } function safeTransferFrom( address from, address to, uint256 tokenId ) public virtual override { super.safeTransferFrom(from, to, tokenId, ""); characterRegistry[tokenId].currentOwner = to; } function setTokenURI(uint256 tokenId, string memory _tokenURI) public { require( _isApprovedOrOwner(_msgSender(), tokenId), "ERC721: transfer caller is not owner nor approved" ); _setTokenURI(tokenId, _tokenURI); } ... }

我们覆盖了上面代码中的transferFrom()和safeTransferFrom()函数。 我们不想实现我们自己的新自定义逻辑。 我们只想在characterRegistry中记录易手情况，以便我们可以跟踪当前所有者和原始所有者。

这就是为什么我们使用super关键字调用transferFrom()和safeTransferFrom()以使用原始传输逻辑，然后将修改当前所有者统计信息的部分添加到 NFT 发送到的地址中的characterRegistry中各自的方法。

在函数原型中使用virtual关键字可以进一步覆盖任何想要继承我们的合约并将其用作其合约的基础的人。

最后，我们定义setTokenURI和tokenURI函数，如下所示。 第一个函数setTokenURI将用于存储指向metadata.json文件的链接。 该文件将在 NFT 铸造后生成。 此 metadata.json 可以托管在您选择的平台上，范围可以从您自己的 Google Drive（不推荐）到 AWS S3 或 GCP 存储桶和 IPFS（首选）。

 contract OurNFTContract is ERC721, ERC721URIStorage, VRFConsumerBaseV2 { ... function setTokenURI(uint256 tokenId, string memory _tokenURI) public { require( _isApprovedOrOwner(_msgSender(), tokenId), "ERC721: transfer caller is not owner nor approved" ); _setTokenURI(tokenId, _tokenURI); } function tokenURI(uint256 tokenId) public view override(ERC721, ERC721URIStorage) returns (string) { require( _isApprovedOrOwner(_msgSender(), tokenId),"Not Permitted"); return super.tokenURI(tokenId); } ... }

我们的智能合约编写阶段到此结束。

在 Polygon Mumbai 测试网上部署和验证

部署和验证部件变得容易。 如果你在 Hardhat 上（我在这个项目中使用它），你可以按照下面提到的步骤

您需要有一个 Polygonscan API 密钥。 注册 Polygon Scan 并创建一个。 创建一个.env文件并添加以下环境变量，如下所示。

 POLYGONSCAN_API_KEY=<YOUR API POLYGONSCAN KEY> MUMBAI_URL=https://polygon-mumbai.g.alchemy.com/v2/<YOUR API POLYGONSCAN KEY> PRIVATE_KEY=<WALLET PRIVATE KEY HERE> REPORT_GAS=true

用您各自的钥匙更换零件。 接下来，打开hardhat.config.js文件并在networks下添加以下内容。

 mumbai: { url: process.env.MUMBAI_URL || "", accounts: [process.env.PRIVATE_KEY] }

如果你在module.exports中没有任何networks属性，那么添加一个这样的。

 networks: { mumbai: { url: process.env.MUMBAI_URL || "", accounts: [process.env.PRIVATE_KEY] } },

此外，您需要在 etherscan 的属性下将 Polygonscan 密钥添加到此etherscan ，如下所示：

 etherscan: { // apiKey: process.env.ETHERSCAN_API_KEY, apiKey: process.env.POLYGONSCAN_API_KEY, },

这应该足够了。

转到您的 Hardhat 项目中的脚本文件夹，然后创建一个名为deploy.js的文件并将以下代码放入其中。

 // We require the Hardhat Runtime Environment explicitly here. This is optional // but useful for running the script in a standalone fashion through `node <script>`. // // When running the script with `npx hardhat run <script>` you'll find the Hardhat // Runtime Environment's members available in the global scope. const hre = require("hardhat"); async function main() { // Hardhat always runs the compile task when running scripts with its command // line interface. // // If this script is run directly using `node` you may want to call compile // manually to make sure everything is compiled // await hre.run('compile'); // We get the contract to deploy const OurNFTContract = await hre.ethers.getContractFactory("OurNFTContract"); const ourNftContract = await OurNFTContract.deploy(414); await ourNftContract.deployed(); console.log("Contract deployed to:", ourNftContract.address); } // We recommend this pattern to be able to use async/await everywhere // and properly handle errors. main().catch((error) => { console.error(error); process.exitCode = 1; });

以上是初始化安全帽项目时提供的样板代码的修改版本。 在第 17 行，我们使用 hardhat 提供的 ethers 中的getContractFactory方法获取我们的合约（名为OurNFTContract ）。 在下一行中，我们使用OurNFTContract.deploy()部署我们的合约。

请务必将414替换为您的订阅 ID。 智能合约上的 deploy 方法采用构造函数参数，在我们的例子中，这将是我们从订阅管理器获得的订阅 ID。

运行npx hardhat run scripts/deploy.js –network mumbai来部署智能合约。 部署后，我们将在控制台上获取合约地址，如下所示：

合约部署完成后，运行npx hardhat verify –network mumbai <Your Deployed Contract Address> <Subscription ID>来验证你的合约。 如果它说它缺少 hardhat-etherscan 模块，只需运行npm I @nomiclabs/hardhat-etherscan然后重新运行验证命令。

您应该得到类似于上图的输出。

添加消费者

验证合同后，返回 VRF 订阅门户并作为消费者添加此合同。 这是重要的一步。 它基本上为你的智能合约创造了一个津贴。 因此，当调用safeMint()并且协调器合约从我们的智能合约发送请求时，它不会失败。 这是保护您的订阅的好方法，因为您的订阅中会有主网 LINK 令牌，这可以防止垃圾邮件。

作为一种良好做法，如果您有不再使用的消费者，则应将其删除。 如果有人无意或有意使用该消费者合同中的 Chainlink VRF 调用，那么您在订阅中的 LINK 余额将用于资助该请求。

铸造 NFT

由于此时我们已经部署并验证了我们的合约，我们可以直接前往 Polygonscan 并尝试铸造 NFT。 我已经这样做了，这是结果。


上图是调用safeMint()函数时发出的事件。 如您所见，它发出了一个请求并发出了一个事件，其中包含一个以 16 开头的requestId （它是一个uint256整数，因此以指数形式表示），调用者的钱包地址以0xe61和名称字符Raze 。


上图显示了从fulfillRandomWords()方法发出的事件，包含requestId （与之前的图像匹配）和返回的两个uint256整数。


上图是铸造的 NFT。 调用了合约上的getCharacter()方法，该方法显示了字符属性。

在 OpenSea 上市

要在 OpenSea 上列出 NFT 合约，只需浏览到testnets.opensea.io/get-listed/step-two ，选择 Mumbai 测试网并输入合约地址。 单击提交以在 OpenSea 上列出您的合同。 从该合约中生成的任何 NFT 都将出现在 OpenSea 上。 如果您在铸造后不通过setTokenURI将metadata.json附加到您的令牌，那将不会很漂亮。

这就是这个人的全部！

我们在这方面做了很多工作。 我们简要讨论了 Chainlink VRF 服务。 我们通过订阅管理器创建了 Chainlink VRF 订阅。 我们编写了一个 NFT 智能合约，它使用 Chainlink VRF v2 为 NFT 提供随机属性，然后将智能合约部署在 Polygon 的孟买测试网上。

此代码已在此GitHub 存储库中开源。

我很想探索如何使用 Chainlink 提供的 Mock VRF Coo​​rdinator 智能合约来测试这样的智能合约，但现在让我们将其推迟到以后的文章中。 让我们在这里结束这个。

如果您喜欢这篇文章，请随时分享并按下那些表达爱意的可爱按钮。 它有助于保持自己的积极性。 如果您有任何主题建议，请在 Twitter 上或通过我的电子邮件与我联系。 在那之前，继续在 Web3 和 WAGMI 上开发很棒的东西！



原文:

… and deploying it on Polygon using Hardhat.

As the title mentions, we will use Chainlink VRF’s version 2 in a smart contract and see how it can be used to add verifiable randomness in a smart contract. While at it, we will create an NFT contract and then deploy it to Mumbai Testnet of Polygon. As a cherry on the top, we will see how to list the minted NFTs from the smart contract on OpenSea. Our NFT images will have no such bounds and can range from your profile picture to a Rare Pokemon or perhaps a mythical devil fruit model but we will not focus on the whole “attaching a metadata.json” part since that’s out of the scope of this article.

This article assumes you are familiar with Alchemy and have a Mumbai Testnet RPC URL for the same or something equivalent to that. Also, you need to have a Polygonscan API key if you want to follow along the verification part. So, for these reasons, feel free browse resources on those topics before you start.

There’s a lot to cover and too few words. So, let’s get on with it.

Chainlink VRF – What and Why?

Chainlink VRF is an offering from Chainlink which provides smart contract a way to introduce randomness into the smart contract. As many readers may be aware of, before VRF, the way to go was using block hash and/or the timestamp or something on those lines to introduce a sense of pseudo randomness.

This was a good first step. But it had a massive apparent flaw. It could be predicted. Because of this, many smart contracts were exploited – especially early-stage games on blockchain which used NFTs or even betting. Since one can effectively time their invocations, they could end up with much higher fortunes. More than being unfair, this caused a dent on the whole ecosystem as Web 3 – something which was supposed to work without trust and be “unexploitable” (at least that’s how it was pitched in the early days) was now not so anymore.

Chainlink took the step to advance the ecosystem further. Sure, enough there were other contenders like Provable (later renamed to Oracalize) but Chainlink VRF outlasted them all and became the go to for introducing off-chain “Verifiable” (hence the name Verifiable Random Function) randomness.

VRF is an oracle which is used for one purpose and one purpose only – getting random number(s). In version 1, smart contract could have queried only 1 random number (uint256) from the oracle. This was altered in the second version where the smart contract could query multiple ones and get an array of uint256 numbers.

The upper limit on the number of random numbers which can be queried at one go is set by the VRF Coordinator Contract on a specific chain. For example, in case of the VRF coordinator contract on Rinkeby, Mumbai and other testnets, the upper limit is 500 at the time of writing.

You may think that this provides a lot of leeway for the developers. But as Uncle Buterin once developed:

“With great data access comes great gas cost”

Chainlink’s documentation details that per uint256 number takes approx. 20k gas. In practice this may vary. And while it might not matter on L2 like Polygon or sidechains and forks like BSC and Avax, in my opinion, it’s a great way to demonstrate responsibility in a gas constrained environment like Eth 1.0.

Moreover, unlike version 1, Chainlink VRF v2 introduces Subscription Manager portal. Basically, you create a subscription here and add funds to it. You get a Subscription ID out of it and that you need to use in your Smart contract as we will see below. Then you add the consumer contract (the address of the contract which will use the Chainlink VRF) and that’s it.

Honestly, the whole flow kind of reminds me of using Chainlink Oracle service to query an API where you need to setFulfillmentPermission().

Creating a Subscription

Before we write the smart contract, we need to create a subscription on the particular chain we want to access Chainlink VRF. This feature on Chainlink VRF is a way to consolidate all smart contracts into one console and is a good step taken in v2 in my opinion.

Head over to the VRF subscription manager portal and click on Create Subscription. This creates a transaction which results in the creation of a subscription. You need to Add Funds in the next step and fund your subscription with LINK tokens.

Once that transaction confirms it will prompt for adding a consumer. This is where we stop for now. You should now have a subscription ID. This will be used by the smart contract so make sure to record it.

Writing the Contract 

// SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT
pragma solidity ^0.8.4;

import "@chainlink/contracts/src/v0.8/interfaces/VRFCoordinatorV2Interface.sol";
import "@chainlink/contracts/src/v0.8/VRFConsumerBaseV2.sol";
import "@openzeppelin/contracts/token/ERC721/ERC721.sol";
import "@openzeppelin/contracts/token/ERC721/extensions/ERC721URIStorage.sol";
import "@openzeppelin/contracts/utils/Counters.sol";

contract OurNFTContract is ERC721, ERC721URIStorage, VRFConsumerBaseV2 {
    using Counters for Counters.Counter;
    VRFCoordinatorV2Interface COORDINATOR;


    uint64 subscriptionId;
    address vrfCoordinator = 0x7a1BaC17Ccc5b313516C5E16fb24f7659aA5ebed;
    bytes32 keyHash = 0x4b09e658ed251bcafeebbc69400383d49f344ace09b9576fe248bb02c003fe9f;
    uint32 callbackGasLimit = 200000;
    uint16 requestConfirmations = 3;
    uint32 numWords =  2;

    struct NFTStat {
        bool exists;
        address originalOwner;
        address currentOwner;
        string characterName;
        uint8 power;
        uint8 specialPower;
        uint16 cooldown;
        uint256 rarity;
    }

    mapping(uint256 => string) requestToCharacterName;
    mapping(uint256 => address) requestToSender;

    Counters.Counter private _tokenIdCounter;


    mapping(uint256 => NFTStat) private characterRegistry;

    event ReceivedRandomness( uint256 reqId, uint256 n1, uint256 n2);
    event RequestedRandomness( uint256 reqId, address invoker, string name);

    constructor(uint64 _subscriptionId) ERC721("OurNFTContract", "ONC") VRFConsumerBaseV2(vrfCoordinator) {
        COORDINATOR = VRFCoordinatorV2Interface(vrfCoordinator);
        subscriptionId = _subscriptionId;
    }

...

}

The contract would not be very complex. The twist in it will be in the minting part. We will use Openzeppelin’s ERC721 contract along with the other needed ones from the package and Chainlink’s Contracts. In this example, we will inherit from ERC721, ERC721URIStorage (used for storing the URI of the respective NFT metadata JSON) and VRFConsumerBaseV2 (throwback to how one needs to inherit ChainlinkClient to query any API using Oracle Contract).

In the lines following up to the constructor, we define a few important variables.

  1. COORDINATOR is used for using the VRF Coordinator (it’s like when you use the IERC20 interface and pass in the ERC20 contract address to invoke a certain function on that ERC20 contract). Here, the COORDINATOR is used to interact with the Coordinator contract on the Mumbai Testnet.
  2. The subscriptionId, vrfCoordinator, keyHash, callbackGasLimit, requestConfirmations, numWords are used for formulating the request we will send to the Coordinator Contract. Most of these can be initialized through the constructor to provide a more dynamic method in contract but we initialize it here to the fixed values. The subscriptionId will be obtained from Subscription Manager while vrfCoordinator and keyHash will vary based on chain. The next three can be modified as per needs and will specify the gas limit of the fulfillment function, number of confirmations the coordinator has to wait and the number of words to send back respectively.
  3. The struct NFTStat is used for tracking the original owner and current owners of the token. Since the NFT will be listed on OpenSea, an immediate function of it would be the ability to sell it. If so, then NFTStat is a handy thing to track the existence, current and original owners, character name, power, special power and cooldown stats. characterRegistry maps each token minted from this character to its respective stat.
  4. requestToCharacterName and requestToSender are handy mappings to have. The minting won’t be instantaneous. When the user invokes the safeMint() function by providing a character name, the function would send a request to the Coordinator contract. The coordinator contract would send back the requested random words through the fulfillRandomWords() methods. Since we won’t have access to the provided character name and the address of the user (msg.sender won’t work inside the fulfillment function), we map these to the request ID which is sent to the coordinator and later access that inside the fulfillRandomWords() function.
  5. _tokenIdCounter tracks the ID of the next token to be minted and is used when minting an NFT.
  6. Once fulfillRandomWords() has completed execution, it will emit out the random words (numbers are called words here) that were received on a particular request ID using the ReceivedRandomness event.

The constructor receives the subscription ID which we obtain from the subscription manager and initialize the coordinator by passing into VRFCoordinatorV2Interface the vrfCoordinator address. This concludes our initial part.

Next, shown in the safeMint() function below, we pass in the name of the character. This is one of the attributes of our NFT. The safeMint() function returns the request ID which is sent to the Coordinator contract. This can help us keep track of the request from the coordinator contract as well.

contract OurNFTContract is ERC721, ERC721URIStorage, VRFConsumerBaseV2 {

...

 // Assumes the subscription is funded sufficiently.
    function safeMint(string calldata name) public returns(uint256 requestId) {
        // Will revert if subscription is not set and funded.
        requestId = COORDINATOR.requestRandomWords(
        keyHash,
        subscriptionId,
        requestConfirmations,
        callbackGasLimit,
        numWords
        );
        requestToCharacterName[requestId] = name;
        requestToSender[requestId] = msg.sender;
        emit RequestedRandomness(requestId, msg.sender, name);
    }

    function fulfillRandomWords(
        uint256 requestId,
        uint256[] memory randomWords
    ) internal override {
        uint256 w1 = randomWords[0];
        uint256 w2 = randomWords[1];
        uint8 p = uint8(w2 % 10);
        uint8 sp = uint8(w2 % 100 /10);
        uint16 c = uint16(w2 % 1000 / 100);


        address sender = requestToSender[requestId];
        string memory name = requestToCharacterName[requestId];
        uint256 tokenId = _tokenIdCounter.current();
        _tokenIdCounter.increment();
        _safeMint(sender, tokenId);

        characterRegistry[tokenId] = NFTStat(
            true,
            sender,
            sender,
            name,
            p,
            sp,
            c,
            w1 % 10000
        );
        emit ReceivedRandomness(requestId, w1, w2);

    }

...
}

One difference some readers might notice above is the change of requestID from bytes32 to uint256. This is one of the nuances of v2 and in my opinion is a subtle but good step. We receive the requestId from the COORDINATOR in the above function and then use it to map the provided character name and minter address in requestToCharacterName and requestToSender mappings. Lastly, the function emits a RequestedRandomness event.

The fulfillRandomness() function above is invoked by the Coordinator and receives the requestId and randomWords array. The former parameter is the same request ID we obtained from invoking safeMint() and the latter contains the 2 random numbers we requested.

It should be noted here that while you can send the request to Coordinator to get random numbers via VRF in any contract method, the fulfillRandomness method with its current function prototype is a must. Otherwise, you would get the “You need to convert your contract to abstract” notification from compiler. This is because the VRFConsumerBaseV2 we inherited makes it mandatory. The coordinator will invoke this function only when sending back the results from VRF service.

This is why here we conduct the actual mint of the NFT. We take out the two random numbers sent back and then calculate the power, special power and cooldown period from the second random number. The last 4 digits of the first random number are used as rarity.

As you may know that using decimal in solidity is not a luxury we have, so the last 4 digits would get converted to a number having two digits to the right of the decimal point. We retrieve back the address of the minter and the character name provided.

We then increment the tokenIdCounter and then mint an NFT with the tokenId to that address. The characterRegistry records the stats we wish to record for this NFT. Lastly, we emit the ReceivedRandomness event which shows us the two uint256 random numbers we received from the VRF for our particular requestId.

The getCharacter() function below is a utility function that gives us the metadata of our minted NFT. This is to be used by the Metadata Server.

contract OurNFTContract is ERC721, ERC721URIStorage, VRFConsumerBaseV2 {

...

function getCharacter(uint256 tokenId)
    public
    view
    returns (
        address,
        address,
        string memory,
        uint8,
        uint8,
        uint16
    ) {
        require(characterRegistry[tokenId].exists == true, "Character does not Exist.");
        return (
            characterRegistry[tokenId].currentOwner,
            characterRegistry[tokenId].originalOwner,
            characterRegistry[tokenId].characterName,
            characterRegistry[tokenId].power,
            characterRegistry[tokenId].specialPower,
            characterRegistry[tokenId].cooldown
        );
    }

...

}

That’s not all. Remember how we wanted to track the stats of our NFT as it changes hands? It is a safe assumption that any decent smart contract would invoke the safeTransferFrom() but some developers might not want to add the extra safeguard of onERC721Received() by implementing the IERC721Receiver interface in their market place (refer to the NFT marketplace article by me). So, we need to override the transferFrom() function also as shown below.

contract OurNFTContract is ERC721, ERC721URIStorage, VRFConsumerBaseV2 {

...

    function transferFrom(
        address from,
        address to,
        uint256 tokenId
    ) public virtual override {
        super.transferFrom(from, to, tokenId);
        characterRegistry[tokenId].currentOwner = to;
    }

    function safeTransferFrom(
        address from,
        address to,
        uint256 tokenId
    ) public virtual override {
        super.safeTransferFrom(from, to, tokenId, "");
        characterRegistry[tokenId].currentOwner = to;
    }

    function setTokenURI(uint256 tokenId, string memory _tokenURI) public {
        require(
            _isApprovedOrOwner(_msgSender(), tokenId),
            "ERC721: transfer caller is not owner nor approved"
        );
        _setTokenURI(tokenId, _tokenURI);
    }

...

}

We override the transferFrom() and safeTransferFrom() functions in the code above. We do not want to implement our own new custom logic. We just want to record the changing of hands in our characterRegistry so that we can keep track of the current owner and original owner.

This is why we use the super keyword to invoke the transferFrom() and safeTransferFrom() to use the original transfer logic and then add the part which modifies the stats of current owner to the address the NFT is being sent to in the characterRegistry in the respective methods.

Using the virtual keyword in the function prototype allows for further overriding might anyone want to inherit our contract and use it as a base for their contract.

Lastly, we define the setTokenURI and tokenURI functions as shown below. The first function setTokenURI will be used to store the link to the metadata.json file. This file will be generated after the NFT is minted. The tokenURI function will be invoked by OpenSea to get the URL of the metadata.json file. This metadata.json can be hosted on a platform of your choice and can range from your own Google Drive (not recommended) to AWS S3 or GCP storage bucket and the IPFS (preferred).

contract OurNFTContract is ERC721, ERC721URIStorage, VRFConsumerBaseV2 {

...

    function setTokenURI(uint256 tokenId, string memory _tokenURI) public {
        require(
            _isApprovedOrOwner(_msgSender(), tokenId),
            "ERC721: transfer caller is not owner nor approved"
        );
        _setTokenURI(tokenId, _tokenURI);
    }

    function tokenURI(uint256 tokenId)
        public
        view
        override(ERC721, ERC721URIStorage)
        returns (string)
    {
        require( _isApprovedOrOwner(_msgSender(), tokenId),"Not Permitted");
        return super.tokenURI(tokenId);
    }

...

}

This concludes our smart contract writing phase.

Deploying and Verifying on Polygon Mumbai Testnet

Deploying and Verifying parts come easy. If you are on Hardhat (I am using it in this project), you can follow the steps mentioned below

You need to have a Polygonscan API key. Sign up on Polygon Scan and create one. Create a .env file and add the following environment variables as shown below.

POLYGONSCAN_API_KEY=<YOUR API POLYGONSCAN KEY>
MUMBAI_URL=https://polygon-mumbai.g.alchemy.com/v2/<YOUR API POLYGONSCAN KEY>
PRIVATE_KEY=<WALLET PRIVATE KEY HERE>
REPORT_GAS=true

Replace the parts with your respective keys. Next, open the hardhat.config.js file and under networks add the following.

    mumbai: {
      url: process.env.MUMBAI_URL || "",
      accounts: [process.env.PRIVATE_KEY]
    }

If you do not have any networks properties in module.exports then add one like this.

  networks: {
    mumbai: {
      url: process.env.MUMBAI_URL || "",
      accounts: [process.env.PRIVATE_KEY]
    }
  },

Also, you need to add your Polygonscan key to this module.exports under the property of etherscan like this:

  etherscan: {
    // apiKey: process.env.ETHERSCAN_API_KEY,
    apiKey: process.env.POLYGONSCAN_API_KEY,
  },

This should be enough.

Go to the scripts folder in your Hardhat project and then create a file named deploy.js and put the following code in it.

// We require the Hardhat Runtime Environment explicitly here. This is optional
// but useful for running the script in a standalone fashion through `node <script>`.
//
// When running the script with `npx hardhat run <script>` you'll find the Hardhat
// Runtime Environment's members available in the global scope.
const hre = require("hardhat");

async function main() {
  // Hardhat always runs the compile task when running scripts with its command
  // line interface.
  //
  // If this script is run directly using `node` you may want to call compile
  // manually to make sure everything is compiled
  // await hre.run('compile');

  // We get the contract to deploy
  const OurNFTContract = await hre.ethers.getContractFactory("OurNFTContract");
  const ourNftContract = await OurNFTContract.deploy(414);

  await ourNftContract.deployed();

  console.log("Contract deployed to:", ourNftContract.address);
}

// We recommend this pattern to be able to use async/await everywhere
// and properly handle errors.
main().catch((error) => {
  console.error(error);
  process.exitCode = 1;
});

The above is a modified version of the boilerplate code provided when you initialize a hardhat project. On line number 17 we are fetching our contract (named OurNFTContract) using the getContractFactory method from ethers provided by hardhat. In the next line we deploy our contract with OurNFTContract.deploy().

Be sure to replace the 414 with your subscription ID. The deploy method on the smart contract takes the constructor arguments which in our case will be the subscription ID we get from our subscription manager.

Run npx hardhat run scripts/deploy.js –network mumbai to deploy the smart contract. Once deployed, we will get the contract address on our console like this:

After the contract is deployed, run npx hardhat verify –network mumbai <Your Deployed Contract Address> <Subscription ID> to verify your contract. In case it says it’s missing the hardhat-etherscan module just run npm I @nomiclabs/hardhat-etherscan and then re-run the verification command.

You should get an output resembling the image above.

Adding a Consumer

Once you have verified your contract, head back to the VRF Subscription Portal and add in this contract as a Consumer. This is an important step. It basically creates an allowance for your smart contract. So, when safeMint() is invoked and the Coordinator contract is sent a request from our smart contract it won’t fail. It’s a good way to safe guard your subscription as you would have mainnet LINK tokens in your subscription and this would prevent spamming.

As a good practice, if you have a consumer that you no longer use, you should remove it. If anyone unintentionally or intentionally uses invokes Chainlink VRF from that consumer contract, then your LINK balance on the Subscription will be used to fund that request.

Minting an NFT

Since we have deployed and verified our contract at this point, we can just head over to Polygonscan and try to mint an NFT. I have already done so and here are the results.


The above image is of the event which was emitted when safeMint() function was invoked. As you can see it sent out a request and emitted an event which contained a requestId starting with 16 (its a uint256 integer and hence has been represented in an exponential form), the wallet address of the invoker starting with 0xe61 and the name of the character Raze.


The above image shows the event emitted from fulfillRandomWords() method and contains requestId (which is matching with the image before) and the two uint256 integers returned.


The above image is of the NFT which was minted. The getCharacter() method on the contract was invoked which shows the character properties.

Listing on OpenSea

To list the NFT contract on OpenSea, just browse to testnets.opensea.io/get-listed/step-two, select the Mumbai testnet and enter the contract address. Click on submit to list your contract on OpenSea. Any NFTs minted from this contract will show up on OpenSea. It won’t be pretty if you do not attach a metadata.json to your token by setTokenURI after minting it though.

That’s all Folks for this one!

We covered a lot of ground on this one. We discussed briefly Chainlink VRF service. We created a Chainlink VRF subscription via the subscription manager. We wrote a NFT smart contract which used Chainlink VRF v2 to provide random properties to the NFT and then deployed the smart contract on Polygon’s Mumbai Testnet.

The code for this has been open sourced at this GitHub repo.

I would have loved to explore how to test such a smart contract using the Mock VRF Coordinator smart contract provided by Chainlink but let’s postpone that to a future article for now. Let’s end this one here.

If you liked the article, please feel free to share and press those lovely buttons which show the love. It helps to keep myself motivated. If you have any topic recommendations, reach out to me on Twitter or through my email. Until then, keep developing awesome stuff on Web3 and WAGMI!
item.author: abhikbanerjee99

item.title: 自下而上的 SaaS 订阅方法——企业以较低成本进行创新的一种方式 | Bottom-up approach to SaaS subscriptions - a way for Enterprises to innovate at a lower cost
item.description:
如今，大公司正在寻找负担得起的高科技解决方案，以管理其广泛的工作流程。 拥有大量预算的企业能够从不断扩大的市场中购买顶级软件； 然而，当时机成熟时，公司代表正在讨论他们的选择，所以他们会立即加入的机会非常低。 相反，他们将花费几个月的时间来评估最佳行动方案。 然而，企业在按需软件方面是否应该如此谨慎？

当一个组织及其员工开始优先考虑相同的事情时，例如提高生产力的不同方法以提高公司的整体绩效，是时候寻找简单的方法来提高绩效，同时花费更少的努力，无论是否有目的在员工的工作流程或公司本身。 这就是 SaaS 订阅受到质疑的时候。 借助 SaaS，企业可以消除与管理和维护昂贵的内部服务器相关的费用和责任。 同时，他们可以更轻松地跟上新的补丁和功能，加快他们自己的软件部署和配置，减少对额外 IT 人员的依赖，并让内部专业人员专注于更具战略性的问题。

企业员工对付费 SaaS 工具的使用应该比目前更容易和更频繁地进行试验。 如果您不是 SaaS 工具的最终用户，则可能很难评估它们的实用性。 高层管理者应该记住，为了使解决方案被认为是成功的，它需要减轻所有用户遇到的问题。 在决定购买基于云的数字工具的订阅时，应该更加信任有价值的最终用户（员工），以便实现这一目标。

有人会说，人类取得进步的原因是人们变得越来越懒惰，而我们如此专注于寻找最简单、最有效的方法来完成我们的日常职责这一事实是一个主要的动力。

以下是依赖 SaaS 工具公司自下而上的方法最终会导致组织创新水平提高的一些原因：

  • 最好让员工寻找“破解”系统并改善他们的结果，同时减少他们所需的设备，因为公司只能从提高生产力和充分发挥员工潜力中受益. 这是收益递增的完美例子，这意味着公司可以用相对较低的投入水平生产更高的产出水平。 如果员工不仅能够以最快的方式完成特定目标，而且能够使用他们认为方便的工具和软件，他们将体验到更高水平的流程参与度。 技术对员工完成工作的能力和工作给他们带来的满意度都有重大影响。

今天的人们对日常使用的技术具有高度选择性，他们选择在工作中使用的业务应用程序也不例外。 他们对包括工作场所在内的生活各个方面的应用程序抱有很高的期望，并且可以利用其丰富的用户体验轻松驾驭 SaaS 应用程序市场。 因此，雇主应鼓励员工寻找新的方法来提高他们完成任务的效率，并为业务成果的整体改善做出贡献。

  • 沟通和内部销售链中的第一个环节是在工作过程中遇到低效率和困难的工人。 包括销售经理在内的员工通常是第一个遇到需要解决的问题的人； 因此，他们更有可能设计出最有创意的方法来解决这个问题。 由于它们是整个内部沟通链的基础，因此他们更有可能发现并启动一个完美的解决方案，以解决他们和企业本身所面临的痛苦。

鉴于当前业务环境的复杂性，最好尽可能准确、尽可能快地做出战略决策。 即使公司管理层意识到了这个问题，无疑也需要大量的时间来找到解决方案，就有利可图的条款进行谈判并将其付诸实施。 为什么不直接跳到以低成本、以更高效的方式解决问题的核心部分，而不必与公司的官僚作风打交道呢？ 在考虑订阅 SaaS 时，毫无疑问，最好的做法是在开始时咨询最终用户，而不是向他们强加未知产品。 员工希望感觉好像他们对所使用的工具有一定的控制权，并且他们不希望被迫使用只满足他们部分要求的系统。 每个人都想要同类中最好的解决方案。

所有员工之间的开放式沟通和协作解决问题确保了重大项目的顺利进行，并按时实现了目标。 同时，到达链条的顶端总是要付出代价——**跨国和代理成本**来驾驭公司官僚机构。 许多创新不仅会在“内部斗争”期间丢失，而且会在一开始就丢失，因为官僚结构会通过降低企业的敏捷性和降低运营效率而无意中扼杀创新。

  • 正如我已经说过的，企业在制定战略决策时必须迅速采取行动。 通过 P2P 连接和信息共享，员工和最终用户可以更快地找到最佳解决方案，这就是为什么动手体验是评估 SaaS 的最佳方式。

在承诺付费订阅之前，大多数（如果不是全部）SaaS 平台都会为用户提供免费试用期，在此期间他们可以测试大部分或所有可用工具。 但是，像企业这样的大型生态系统无法承担参与这些试验的费用。 但是，如果我们问那些尝试过它们的人呢？

世界各地的公司都在转向 SaaS 解决方案来满足广泛的业务需求，而其他公司的经验可以帮助避免反复试验。 持续的信息流对于成长中的企业至关重要，因为它是避免耗时的决策过程和由严格的多级层次结构造成的不必要的公司官僚主义的好方法。 在最好的任务管理工具领域，知识共享很少是自上而下发生的，而且上层利益相关者的数量往往较少。 大多数对现状和工作中的痛苦不满意的人也在寻找解决方案，以找到最能满足他们需求的解决方案。 感兴趣的员工之间的网络可能是新的 SaaS 解决方案的重要来源，这些解决方案已经过评估和测试，其实施将促进企业创新。

哪些误解阻碍了公司寻找有用的 SaaS？

只有少数人反对使用 SaaS 工具的自下而上方法，但仍然必须解决这些问题，因为这种短视的观点阻碍了业务增长。

  • 与利益相关者可能认为的相反，发现和实施新的 SaaS 工具不会导致成本上升或无法控制。

企业偶尔会遇到冗余、超支和无效的解决方案，但这本质上等同于投资创新，总是涉及风险和费用。 然而，自下而上的方法与自上而下的方法相比，这种成本预计要低得多（这也带来了错误决策的风险）。 如前所述，底层的最终用户几乎总是为自己或一小群人购买一个基于云的订阅，试图解决他们正在努力解决的一些问题。 此外，如果该工具被越来越多的人广泛采用，它很有可能解决公司范围内的需求，因此应该考虑企业级订阅 - 创新投资产生的风险最低，成功的可能性最高。

  • 另一种观点是，使用基于 Web 的工具会增加安全风险，考虑到 SaaS 业务扩展的速度和所使用的应用程序的多样性，这是一个更合理的担忧。 这只是为企业业务投资适当且安全的基于云的软件的另一个理由。 允许在采购和 IT 安全流程之外不受限制地购买 SaaS 许可证的论点也是有缺陷的。 如前所述，员工自然会尝试减轻他们的痛点。 因此，限制他们使用购买工具只会增加他们对免费工具的使用，而免费工具通常不太安全。

一个很好的例子就是翻译任何与工作相关的东西的反复要求——一个来自我目前工作的行业的实际例子。你有没有想过你的员工如何在没有付费安全工具的情况下进行管理——今天的翻译需求？ 他们只是利用免费的在线机器翻译服务（例如 Google Translate 或 DeepL）在线复制和粘贴敏感内容甚至整个文件以获得翻译。 有没有人可以争辩说它以任何方式都不安全？ 尽管企业可以通过使用免费的在线翻译工具来节省资金，但他们也有可能危及自己的声誉。

结论
不订阅 SaaS 服务或对员工做出的 SaaS 选择表现出不信任是一种虚假的经济，它会阻碍工作流程显着提高效率，并且通常会导致企业内部缺乏创新。

在现代商业世界中，公司必须能够迅速转移注意力，以利用新兴机遇或应对不可预见的挑战。 这是通过订阅软件即服务 (SaaS) 实现的，它使用户可以自由地尝试新方法、实施新服务，甚至测试其他供应商，以便找到最适合他们需求的解决方案.

* https://ascendixtech.com/number-saas-companies-statistics * https://ascendixtech.com/number-saas-companies-statistics



原文:

Nowadays, large companies are looking for affordable high-tech solutions in order to manage their extensive workflows. Enterprises that operate with sizable budgets are in a position to purchase top-tier software from the expanding market; however, when comes the time to make, company representatives are debating their options, so the chances they will jump right in are extremely low. Instead, they will spend several months evaluating the best course of action. Should enterprises, however, be so cautious when it comes to on-demand software?

When an organisation and its employees begin prioritising the same things, such as different methods of increasing productivity in order to improve the overall performance of the company, it is time to look for simple ways to improve performance while expending less effort, whether it is aimed at the employees' workflow or for the company itself. And that is when a SaaS subscription comes into question. With SaaS, businesses can eliminate the expenses and responsibilities associated with managing and maintaining costly in-house servers. At the same time, they can keep up with new patches and features more easily, accelerate their own software deployments and configurations, reduce their reliance on additional IT staff, and free up in-house professionals to focus on more strategic concerns.

The use of paid SaaS tools by employees of an enterprise should be made easier and experimented with more frequently than is currently the case. If you are not the end-user of the SaaS tools, it can be difficult to evaluate their usefulness. Top managers should keep in mind that in order for a solution to be considered successful, it needs to alleviate the problems experienced by all users. When it comes to making a decision about purchasing subscriptions to cloud-based digital tools, there should be much more trust placed in the hands of the end-users of value (employees) so that this goal can be accomplished.

As one would say, the reason humanity is making progress is that people are getting lazier, and the fact that we are so focused on finding the easiest and most efficient ways to complete our daily responsibilities is a major motivator.

*What are the benefits of SaaS subscriptions for enterprises?
*
Here are some of the reasons why relying on a bottom-up approach with SaaS tool companies will ultimately lead to an increase in the organization's level of innovation:

  • It would be best to provide employees looking for ways to ‘hack’ the system and improve their results while doing less with the equipment they require since the company can only benefit from an increase in productivity as well as the full realisation of the workers' potential. It is the perfect example of increasing returns, which means that a company can produce higher output levels with relatively lower levels of input. If a worker were able to accomplish a particular objective not only in the quickest way possible but also with tools and software that they found to be convenient, they would experience a higher level of engagement with the process. Technology has a significant impact on both the capacity of an employee to complete their work and the satisfaction that work brings them.

People today are highly selective when it comes to the technology they use on a daily basis, and the business applications they choose to use at work are no exception. They have high expectations for applications in all aspects of their lives, including the workplace, and can easily navigate the SaaS application market by leveraging their extensive user experience. Therefore, employers should encourage their employees to look for new ways to improve the efficiency with which they complete their tasks and contribute to the overall improvement of business results.

  • The first link in the chain of communication and internal selling is workers who experience inefficiencies and difficulties in the course of their work. Employees, including sales managers, are typically the first ones to encounter a problem that needs to be addressed; consequently, it is more likely that they will devise the most creative approach trying to resolve the issue. Due to the fact that they serve as a foundation for the entire in-house communication chain, it is more likely that they will discover and initiate a perfect solution that targets the pains that they, along with the business itself, struggle with.

Given the complexity of the current business environment, it is better to make strategic decisions as precisely as possible and as quickly as possible. Even if the management of the company recognises the issue, it will undoubtedly take a significant amount of time to locate a solution, negotiate the terms of a profitable one, and put it into action. Why not just skip ahead to the part where the issue is resolved at its core for a low cost, in a more productive manner, and without having to deal with the bureaucracy of the corporation? When a SaaS subscription is being considered, it is unquestionably the best course of action to consult with end-users at the beginning rather than imposing an unknown product on them. Employees want to feel as though they have some control over the tools they use, and they don't want to be coerced into utilising a system that meets only some of their requirements. Everyone wants solutions that are the best of their kind.

Open communication and collaborative problem-solving among all employees ensure that major projects keep progressing in a fluid manner and that goals are met on time. Meanwhile, reaching the top of the chain always comes at a cost -** transnational and agency costs** to navigate the corporate bureaucracy. A lot of innovation will be lost not only during the "internal fight" but also right at the start as bureaucratic structures can unintentionally stifle innovation by making businesses less agile and decreasing operational efficiency.

  • As I already said, it is essential for businesses to act quickly when making strategic decisions. Through P2P connections and information sharing, employees and end users can find the best solutions much faster, which is why hands-on experience is the best way to evaluate SaaS.

Before committing to a paid subscription, most, if not all, SaaS platforms provide users with a free trial period during which they can test out most or all of the available tools. However, a large ecosystem like an enterprise cannot afford to take part in those tryouts. But what if we asked those who tried them all?

“The percentage of companies using SaaS to meet their software needs is steadily increasing, with only 38% of companies relying on SaaS in 2016 and 51% in 2018. The percentage of companies relying solely on SaaS is projected to hit 80% in 2022.”*

Companies around the world are turning to SaaS solutions for a wide range of business needs, and the experience of other companies can help avoid trial and error. A constant flow of information is essential for growing businesses as it is a great way to avoid a time-consuming decision-making process and unnecessary corporate bureaucracy caused by a strict multi-level hierarchy. In the field of best task management tools, knowledge sharing rarely occurs from the top down, and the number of stakeholders at the top is frequently smaller. Most people who are dissatisfied with the status quo and the pains they are experiencing at work are also searching for solutions in order to find the best one to meet their needs. Networking among interested employees could be a significant source of new SaaS solutions that have already been evaluated and tested and whose implementation would boost enterprise innovation.

What are the misconceptions that prevent companies from finding useful SaaS?

There are only a few arguments against the bottom-up approach with SaaS tools, but they must still be addressed because this myopic perspective impedes business growth.

  • Discovering and implementing new SaaS tools does not incur escalating or uncontrollable costs, contrary to what stakeholders may believe.

Enterprises will occasionally encounter redundancy, overspent and ineffective solutions, but this is essentially equivalent to investing in innovation, which always involves risk and expense. This cost, however, is anticipated to be significantly lower for bottom-up versus top-down approaches (which also comes with the risk of wrong decisions). As previously stated, end-users at the bottom almost always purchase a single cloud-based subscription for themselves or a small group of people, attempting to solve some problems they are struggling with. Moreover, if the tool is widely adopted by a growing group of people, there is a good chance that it solves a company-wide need, and therefore the Enterprise level subscription should be considered - the investment in innovation has yielded the lowest risks and the highest likelihood of success.

  • The other belief is that the use of web-based tools increases security risk, which is a more valid concern given the rate of SaaS business expansion and the variety of apps employed. That is just one more reason to invest in appropriate and secure cloud-based software for enterprise businesses. The argument that permitting unrestricted purchasing of SaaS licences outside of the procurement and IT security processes is also flawed. As aforementioned, employees naturally attempt to alleviate their pain points. Therefore, restricting their access to purchasing tools will only increase their use of free tools, which are commonly less secure.

A good illustration of this would be the recurring requirement to translate anything work-related - an actual example from the industry I currently work in. Have you ever given any thought to how your employees manage without the benefit of paid secure tools for their day-to-day translation needs? (And almost all businesses do not have those). They simply make use of free online machine translation services such as Google Translate or DeepL to copy and paste sensitive content or even entire files online in order to obtain the translation. Is there anyone who could argue that it is not secure in any way? Even though businesses can save money by using free online translation tools, they risk jeopardising their reputation.

Conclusion
Not subscribing to a SaaS service or showing mistrust in the SaaS choices made by your employees is a false economy that prevents a workflow that is significantly more efficient and often leads to a lack of innovation within an enterprise.

In the modern world of business, it is essential for companies to be able to quickly shift their focus in order to capitalise on emerging opportunities or address unforeseen challenges. This is made possible by subscription software as a service (SaaS), which gives users the freedom to experiment with new methods, implement new services, or even test out other vendors in order to find the solutions that are the most ideally suited to their requirements.

*https://ascendixtech.com/number-saas-companies-statistics
item.author: dima_litvinov41

item.title: 让死鱼游泳 - 测试驱动 Golang | Make The Dead Fish Swim - Test Driven Golang
item.description:
今天我们来看看如何解决“让死鱼游泳”——一个排名为“6 kyu”的 Codewars 挑战，我们将使用测试驱动开发来完成它。

首先，让我们看看我们将要解决的挑战：

 Write a simple parser that will parse and run Deadfish . Deadfish has 4 commands , each 1 character long : i increments the value ( initially 0 ) d decrements the value s squares the value o outputs the value into the return array Invalid characters should be ignored . Parse ( "iiisdoso" ) == [] int { 8 , 64 } // example output

一个快速的免责声明，很有可能有一个非常简洁和聪明的解决方案来解决这个挑战，我将在这篇文章的最后分享它。

但是，我的目标是练习解决问题，并将其分解为可在其他地方使用的可测试的小功能块（如果我们在现实生活中构建某些东西）。

我们将在两个不同的文件中工作， main.go和main_test.go

我还将使用我在编写测试时喜欢使用的包，该包是https://github.com/stretchr/testify 。

对我来说，一个好的起点是为整个程序中重复的挑战位编写一些测试函数，递增一，递减一，平方并输出到数组。 虽然现在，我将从前三个开始。

 func TestIncrementOne ( t * testing . T ) { assert . Equal ( t , 1 , IncrementOne ( 0 )) assert . Equal ( t , 5 , IncrementOne ( 4 )) assert . Equal ( t , 100 , IncrementOne ( 99 )) } func TestDecrementOne ( t * testing . T ) { assert . Equal ( t , 0 , DecrementOne ( 1 )) assert . Equal ( t , 4 , DecrementOne ( 5 )) assert . Equal ( t , 99 , DecrementOne ( 100 )) } func TestSquare ( t * testing . T ) { assert . Equal ( t , 1 , Square ( 1 )) assert . Equal ( t , 25 , Square ( 5 )) assert . Equal ( t , 64 , Square ( 8 )) }

这些函数中的每一个都将为我们提供一个特定的功能，我们稍后在解析挑战的输入时将需要这些功能。 让我们运行测试。

 // go test ./main_test.go:23:21: undefined: IncrementOne ./main_test.go:27:21: undefined: DecrementOne ./main_test.go:31:21: undefined: Square FAIL github.com/damiensedgwick/codewars/makeTheDeadFishSwim [build failed]

很简单，它们都失败了，因为实际上还没有任何功能存在，所以让我们创建它们。

 func IncrementOne ( n int ) int { return n + 1 } func DecrementOne ( n int ) int { return n - 1 } func Square ( n int ) int { return n * n }

这些功能非常简单，实际上可能看起来微不足道，但它们将为我们提供解决挑战所需的基础。

让我们再次运行测试。

 // go test PASS ok github.com/damiensedgwick/codewars/makeTheDeadFishSwim 0.195s

好多了，接下来呢。

我认为一个好的步骤是获取我们将要接收的输入字符串，并将其转换为字符串数组，以便我们可以循环遍历它并决定我们希望对该数组的每个元素发生什么。

考试

func TestSplitString ( t * testing . T ) { assert . Equal ( t , [] string { "i" , "i" , "i" , "s" , "d" , "o" , "s" , "o" , }, SplitString ( "iiisdoso" )) }

如果我们运行go test它会失败，因为这个函数还不存在，所以让我们创建它。

 func SplitString ( s string ) [] string { return strings . Split ( s , "" ) }

现在我们的测试再次满意，我们可以继续下一步，实际上可能是解析输入。 让我们编写一个测试，看看我们是如何进行的。

 func TestParse ( t * testing . T ) { assert . Equal ( t , [] int { 8 , 64 }, Parse ( "iiisdoso" )) }

挑战很好，可以给我们一个测试用例，所以我们用它来编写测试，现在我们可以尝试创建函数并让测试通过。

 func Parse ( input string ) [] int { var output = [] int {} count := 0 s := SplitString ( input ) for _ , v := range s { switch v { case "i" : count = IncrementOne ( count ) case "d" : count = DecrementOne ( count ) case "s" : count = Square ( count ) case "o" : output = append ( output , count ) default : continue } } return output }

所以现在我们有了解析函数，我们可以再次运行测试，他们应该都很高兴。

测试很开心，所以我将把我的代码粘贴到 codewars 挑战中，确保调整我为他们指定的变量名称使用的变量名称，并导入我使用的任何包。

让我们来看看结果

Test Results: Random tests random test #1 (`ossrshsbzotsdkpvuisekvcci`) random test #2 (`odzosiqdshbiikiisiiyigdlzyd`) random test #3 (`sokssndofoimuqbqipsedfodticuotosdso`) random test #4 (`siiooisdisijoilusdshqddzosiotdoio`) random test #5 (`oiiswwmaisdosisooitio`) random test #6 (`sdhhidyoioimsoidioooodsiddoio`) ... random test #94 (`dssdbiddoskuoondgirikdeuolspdjtsjkisido`) random test #95 (`rsoboobssixzzoudfiinbddgm`) random test #96 (`iooeioyoyviwsoqsisdhaixxl`) random test #97 (`idsdodsfsdbxdismiskdsis`) random test #98 (`icdzdsissuigoijvopdddpiddgsw`) random test #99 (`diioidooooisssmdiiisiiosossiiohivvowud`) random test #100 (`odooszoatitdwdibkdko`)

所有测试用例均已通过，挑战完成！ 现在如前所述，对于这个挑战可能有一个非常简短的答案，我将在下面分享，但我希望这篇文章能让您了解测试驱动开发的强大功能。

 package kata func Parse ( data string ) [] int { output := [] int {} var val int for _ , s := range data { switch string ( s ) { case "i" : val ++ case "d" : val -- case "s" : val = val * val case "o" : output = append ( output , val ) } } return output }

因此，实际上该解决方案与我们在本文中实施的解决方案非常相似。 唯一的区别是我们有我们的测试支持，让我们对我们的程序功能有绝对的信心。



原文:

Today we are going to take a look at solving "Make The Dead Fish Swim" - A Codewars challenge ranked '6 kyu' and we are going to do it using Test Driven Development.

First of all, let us look at the challenge we are going to tackle:

Write a simple parser that will parse and run Deadfish. Deadfish has 4 commands, each 1 character long: i increments the value (initially 0) d decrements the value s squares the value o outputs the value into the return array Invalid characters should be ignored. Parse("iiisdoso") == []int{8, 64} // example output

A quick disclaimer, there is a good chance that there is a very concise and clever solution to this challenge, and I will share it at the end of this post.

However my goal here is to practice taking a problem, and breaking it down into small testable pieces of functionality that could be used elsewhere (if we were building something in real life).

We are going to be working in two different files, main.go and main_test.go

I will also be using a package that I like to use when writing tests and that package is https://github.com/stretchr/testify.

For me, a good starting point is to write out some test functions for the bits of the challenge that are repeated throughout the program, increment one, decrement one, square and outputs to the array. Although for now, I will start with the first three.

func TestIncrementOne(t *testing.T) { assert.Equal(t, 1, IncrementOne(0)) assert.Equal(t, 5, IncrementOne(4)) assert.Equal(t, 100, IncrementOne(99))
} func TestDecrementOne(t *testing.T) { assert.Equal(t, 0, DecrementOne(1)) assert.Equal(t, 4, DecrementOne(5)) assert.Equal(t, 99, DecrementOne(100))
} func TestSquare(t *testing.T) { assert.Equal(t, 1, Square(1)) assert.Equal(t, 25, Square(5)) assert.Equal(t, 64, Square(8))
}

Each of these functions will provide us with a specific piece of functionality that we will later need when parsing the input for the challenge. Let us run the tests.

// go test

./main_test.go:23:21: undefined: IncrementOne
./main_test.go:27:21: undefined: DecrementOne
./main_test.go:31:21: undefined: Square
FAIL    github.com/damiensedgwick/codewars/makeTheDeadFishSwim [build failed]

Pretty simple, they all fail because none of the functions actually exist yet, so let us create them.

func IncrementOne(n int) int { return n + 1
} func DecrementOne(n int) int { return n - 1
} func Square(n int) int { return n * n
}

These functions are very simple and may seem in fact trivial however they are going to give us the foundations we need to solve the challenge.

Let us run the tests again.

// go test

PASS
ok      github.com/damiensedgwick/codewars/makeTheDeadFishSwim  0.195s

Much better, so what is next.

I think a good step would be to take the input string we are going to receive, and turn it in to an array of strings so that we can loop through it and decide what we would like to happen for each element of that array.

The test

func TestSplitString(t *testing.T) { assert.Equal(t, []string{ "i", "i", "i", "s", "d", "o", "s", "o", }, SplitString("iiisdoso"))
}

If we run go test it will fail because this function does not yet exist, so let us create it.

func SplitString(s string) []string { return strings.Split(s, "")
}

Now our tests are happy again, we can proceed to the next step which could in fact be parsing the input. Let us write a test and see how we get on.

func TestParse(t *testing.T) { assert.Equal(t, []int{8, 64}, Parse("iiisdoso"))
}

The challenge is kind enough to give us a test case so we have used that to write the test and now we can try to create the function and make the test pass.

func Parse(input string) []int { var output = []int{} count := 0 s := SplitString(input) for _, v := range s { switch v { case "i": count = IncrementOne(count) case "d": count = DecrementOne(count) case "s": count = Square(count) case "o": output = append(output, count) default: continue } } return output
}

So now we have our parse function, we can run the tests again and they should all be happy.

The tests are happy so I am going to take my code and paste it in to the codewars challenge, making sure to tweak variables names I used for the ones they specified and importing any packages that I used.

Let us take a look at the results

Test Results:
Random tests
random test #1 (`ossrshsbzotsdkpvuisekvcci`)
random test #2 (`odzosiqdshbiikiisiiyigdlzyd`)
random test #3 (`sokssndofoimuqbqipsedfodticuotosdso`)
random test #4 (`siiooisdisijoilusdshqddzosiotdoio`)
random test #5 (`oiiswwmaisdosisooitio`)
random test #6 (`sdhhidyoioimsoidioooodsiddoio`)
...
random test #94 (`dssdbiddoskuoondgirikdeuolspdjtsjkisido`)
random test #95 (`rsoboobssixzzoudfiinbddgm`)
random test #96 (`iooeioyoyviwsoqsisdhaixxl`)
random test #97 (`idsdodsfsdbxdismiskdsis`)
random test #98 (`icdzdsissuigoijvopdddpiddgsw`)
random test #99 (`diioidooooisssmdiiisiiosossiiohivvowud`)
random test #100 (`odooszoatitdwdibkdko`)

All test cases have passed and the challenge is complete! Now as mentioned, there is probably a very short and concise answer to this challenge which I will share below however I hope this post has given you an insight to how powerful test driven development can be.

package kata func Parse(data string) []int{ output := []int{} var val int for _, s := range data { switch string(s) { case "i": val++ case "d": val-- case "s": val = val * val case "o": output = append(output, val) } } return output
}

So in fact the solution is very similar to the one we implemented in this post. The only difference is we have the backing of our tests to give us absolute confidence in our programs functionality.
item.author: damiensedgwick

item.title: 我在教 JavaScript 时发现难以解释的五个概念（第 1 部分） | Five concepts I found difficult to explain when teaching JavaScript (part 1)
item.description:
不久前，我收到一个朋友的消息，请求我的帮助。 他的目标是学习 JavaScript (JS)。 事实上，他指出他对学习 JS 中的面向对象编程很感兴趣。 他以前有使用 C/C++ 编码的经验，但现在他想跳入一种更“现代”的语言。

一开始我很困惑。 这是第一次有人请求我帮助学习一门新的编程语言。 通常，我会帮助那些在软件工程方面开始职业生涯并且以前很少（或没有）编码经验的人。 而且我总是告诉他们：编程语言不应该是最重要的学习内容（框架也不应该） 。 相反，他们应该学习其背后的概念（算法、数据结构、范式等）。

所以，我告诉我的朋友那里有很多免费的在线资源可以用来学习 JS（我什至向他推荐了我最喜欢的资源）。 但他回答说，他曾尝试自学，但没有成功。 他已经了解了基础知识：变量、语句、循环等，但是在尝试了解什么是回调时卡住了。 更重要的是，他无法理解 OOP 在 JS 中是如何工作的。 所以，我意识到他不是在为编程挑战而苦苦挣扎，他在为JS 概念苦苦挣扎（就像我们许多人在生活的某个阶段所做的一样😊）。

因此，我开始准备一些课程，即使我不认为自己是 JS 专家，以下是我在教朋友 JS 时最挣扎的 5 个概念：

和我的朋友一样，我的职业生涯也是从 C/C++ 开始的。 所以，我记得当我开始将代码组织成函数时的感觉。 我可以从代码的不同位置重用的小（最好）代码片段。 用参数编写函数并从其他函数调用这些函数对我来说简直太棒了（我还记得我当时遇到的链接器错误😊）。 一段时间后，我开始使用 Java 和 C# 进行编码：几乎相同的好感觉（考虑到我不必再面对链接器错误和指针）。

但是当我开始学习 JS 时，我花了一段时间才完全理解什么是回调（我的意思是完全理解）。 将函数作为其他函数的参数的想法……嗯，这对我来说太过分了。 这怎么可能？ 回调是如何存储在内存中的？ 更重要的是：回调到底有什么用？ 我记得所有这些问题都在我脑海中浮现，没有简单的答案。

现在，我可以从我朋友的脸上看出他和我学习回调时的想法一样。 他理解这个概念本身，但他无法理解回调的用处，例如。 于是，我想到了另一个相关的概念：异步编程。 他终于看到了隧道尽头的曙光。 一段时间后，当他了解数组时，他意识到原型的大多数函数都需要一些谓词（另一个函数）作为参数（即回调😊）。

等一下。 原型？ 那是什么呀？ 嗯，那是另一个故事了。

快乐编码！

有用的链接：
https://developer.mozilla.org/es/docs/Glossary/Callback_function https://developer.mozilla.org/es/docs/Glossary/Callback_function



原文:

A while ago, I received a message from a friend asking for my help. He aimed to learn JavaScript (JS). As a matter of fact, he pointed out that he was interested in learning object-oriented programming in JS. He had previous experience coding with C/C++, but now he wanted to jump in into a more “modern” language.

I was quite confused at first. This was the first time someone requested my help for learning a new programming language. Normally, I help people who are starting their professional careers on software engineering and have little (or none) previous experience with coding. And I always tell them the same: the programming language should not be the most important thing to learn (neither the frameworks). Rather, they should learn the concepts behind it (algorithms, data structures, paradigms, etc.).

So, I told my friend there were a lot of free online resources out there for learning JS (I even recommended him my favorite ones). But he replied that he had tried learning on his own, with no success. He had understood the basics: variables, statements, loops, etc. but got stuck when trying to learn what a callback was. More importantly, he was not able to understand how OOP worked in JS. So, I realized that he was not struggling with programming challenges, he was struggling (like many of us have done at some point of our lives 😊) with JS concepts.

Thus, I started preparing some lessons, and even I don’t consider myself a JS expert, here are the 5 concepts I struggled the most with when teaching my friend JS:

1- Callbacks

Like my friend, I also started my career coding with C/C++. So, I remember how well it felt when I started organizing my code into functions. Small (preferably) pieces of code that I could reuse from different places in the code. Writing functions with their arguments and invoking those functions from other functions was for me simply amazing (I also remember the linker errors I got back then 😊). Some time later, I started coding with Java and C#: almost the same good feeling (considering I didn’t have to face the linker errors and pointers anymore).

But when I started learning JS, it took me a while to fully understand what a callback was (I mean fully understand). The idea of having functions as arguments of other functions… Well, that was too much for me. How the heck was that even possible? How were callbacks stored in memory? More importantly: how was a callback useful at all? I remember all those questions coming to my mind with no simple answer.

Now, I could see from my friend’s face that he was thinking the same I did when I was learning callbacks. He understood the concept itself, but he was not able understand a callback’s usefulness, for instance. So, I came to another related concept: asynchronous programming. And he finally saw the light at the end of the tunnel. A time later, when he learnt about arrays, he realized that most of the prototype’s functions expected some predicate (another function) as parameter (i.e. a callback 😊).

Wait a minute. Prototype? What the heck is that? Well, that’s another story.

Happy coding!

Useful links:
https://developer.mozilla.org/es/docs/Glossary/Callback_function
item.author: konohamaru

item.title: 奇怪的柜台 | Hackerrank Problem Solution With Explanation Hackerrank 问题解决方案与解释 | Strange Counter | Hackerrank Problem Solution With Explanation
item.description:
有一个奇怪的计数器。 在第一秒，它显示数字。 每秒显示的数字减 1，直到达到 1。在下一秒，计时器重置为2 x 前一个周期的初始数字并继续倒计时。 下图显示了前三个周期中每个时间t的计数器值：

查找并打印计数器在时间t显示的值。

输入格式
一个整数，即t的值。

样本输入
样本输出
问题链接： https ://www.hackerrank.com/challenges/strange-code/problem

解决方案（Python3）

 def strangeCounter(t): # Write your code here n = math.log(((t + 3) / 3), 2) if math.floor(n) == math.ceil(n): return 1 n = int(math.ceil(n)) term = 3 * (math.pow(2, n) - 1) return int(term - t + 1)

解释

从给定的问题陈述中，我们可以清楚地看到：

完成第一个周期所需的时间： 3s
完成第二个周期所需时间： 6s
完成第 3 个周期所需时间： 12 秒

等等。

如果我们稍微观察一下，我们会发现周期所花费的时间正在形成一个系列，并且该系列在GP（几何级数）中，共同比率为2 。

很明显，任何循环结束的时间，是完成该循环直到该循环所用的所有时间的累积总和。 即。

第一个周期结束的时间： 3
周期结束的时间：完成第一个周期所用的时间 + 完成第二个周期所用的时间 = 3 + 6 = 9

等等。

如果该系列在GP中，则可以使用以下公式计算累积和，即任何周期的结束时间：

其中S是级数之和， a是级数的第一项， r是公比， n是级数中的项数。

在这里，在问题中，我们得到了级数的总和（在这种情况下为t ），我们知道第一项和公比（即3和2 ）。 所以，我们必须计算n 。 使用上面的公式，我们可以推导出：

如果我们得到一个整数作为n ，那么给定t是循环的结束时间。 但是如果我们得到一个浮点数，那么t是上一个周期的结束时间和下一个周期之间的时间。 在这种情况下，使用t的 ceil 值和 GP之和的公式，我们可以计算当前周期的结束时间，比如terms 。 术语和t之间的差异就是我们的答案。



原文:

There is a strange counter. At the first second, it displays the number . Each second, the number displayed by decrements by 1 until it reaches 1. In next second, the timer resets to 2 x the initial number of the prior cycle and continues counting down. The diagram below shows the counter values for each time t in the first three cycles:

Find and print the value displayed by the counter at time t.

Input Format
A single integer, the value of t.

Sample Input
4

Sample Output
6

Link to question: https://www.hackerrank.com/challenges/strange-code/problem

Solution (Python3) 

def strangeCounter(t):
    # Write your code here
    n = math.log(((t + 3) / 3), 2)
    if math.floor(n) == math.ceil(n):
        return 1
    n = int(math.ceil(n))
    term = 3 * (math.pow(2, n) - 1)
    return int(term - t + 1)

Explanation

From the given problem statement, we can clearly see that:

Time taken to complete 1st cycle: 3s
Time taken to complete 2nd cycle: 6s
Time taken to complete 3rd cycle: 12s

And so on.

If we observe a little bit then we will find that time taken by cycles is forming a series and that series is in G.P. (Geometric Progression) with common ratio as 2.

It is clear that the time at which is any cycle ends, is the cumulative sum of all the time taken to complete the cycle till that cycle. Viz.

Time at which 1st cycle ends: 3
Time at which cycle ends: Time taken to complete 1st cycle + Time taken to complete 2nd cycle = 3 + 6 = 9

And so on.

If this series is in G.P. then the cumulative sum, i.e. the end time for any cycle, can be calculated using the below formula:

S = a(rⁿ-1)/(r-1),

where S is the sum of the series, a is the first term of the series, r is the common ratio and n is the number of terms in the series.

Here, in the problem, we are given the sum of the series (i.e. t in this case), we know the first term and the common ratio (i.e. 3 and 2). So, we have to calculate n. Using the above formula, we can deduce:

n=log₂((S+3)/3)

If we get a whole number as n then given t is the end time of the cycle. But if we get a floating number, then t is the time between the end times of previous cycle and next cycle. In this case, using the ceil value of t and the formula of the Sum of G.P. we can calculate the end time for the current cycle, say terms. And the difference between the terms and the t is our answer.

item.title: 赌场博彩网站 | Casino betting sites
item.description:
我。
我被艾瑞莎富兰克林去世的消息惊醒。 所有的尊重都是值得的，我不会重复我对这位非凡的女人和她非凡的才华的钦佩。 我这辈子只有两个艺人能接受任何一首歌，他们的演绎肯定会比原作更上一层楼。 雷查尔斯是第一个。 艾瑞莎富兰克林位居第二。 任何音乐专家都能把最简单的歌曲变成激起群众热情的合唱。 任何人都可以采用福音歌曲、爱国赞美诗、摇滚标准——无论来源如何——并让它们成为自己的声音。

我也无法抗拒强调艾瑞莎的死是对音乐的又一次打击，也是一种随着时间的推移似乎在逐渐减少的真正天赋的诱惑。 埃尔顿·约翰多年来一直是 Autotune 等现代唱片的直言不讳的批评者，他今天早上在推特上写道：“艾瑞莎·富兰克林的去世对任何热爱真正音乐的人都是一个打击：发自内心、灵魂和教会的音乐。独一无二, 他的钢琴演奏被低估了 - 他是我最喜欢的钢琴家之一 betting-sites.in

安息。 灵魂女王

二、

我完全不明白。

昨天我们获悉，300 名天主教神父性侵了 1000 多名儿童。 但真正令人惊讶的是——这种可怕的行为只发生在宾夕法尼亚州！

国家！ 数以百计的宗教恋童癖者。 千名受害者。

我们可以肯定，国界并没有阻止恋童癖。 如果这些恐怖事件发生在宾夕法尼亚州，人们会推测掠食者和受害者在 49 个州和许多其他州可能一样多。 换句话说，它可能只是表面上的一个划痕。

必须要做些事情。 现在。 几十年前就应该做点什么，但一个直接对世界上更多苦难负责的宗教机构，也许是另一个实体，仍然没有受到影响。 执法在哪里？ 教会的道德在哪里？ 我们的集体愤怒在哪里？ 这些建筑物应该关闭并出售。

事实上，必须取回数千亿天主教会资产，其中许多是通过盗窃、恐惧和欺骗获得的，并分配给这个可怕的思想改变机构的受害者。

所有有组织的宗教都是珊瑚和烟幕。 是时候面对群众的无知和我们的集体抵抗了。

我，我，我。

赌场业陷入困境。 它不再生长。 这些数字停滞不前，未来几年的人口变化只会让事情变得更糟。

赌场为自己创造了一切。 企业贪婪。 过度膨胀。 质量饱和度。 停车费。 摇零售。 不合理的度假费。 玩钱的成功率较低。 每个玩家都知道，每个游客都知道。

上半年，拉斯维加斯所有主要赌场的收入都出现了下降——尽管出现了所谓的“繁荣经济”，但还是出现了下降。 本周早些时候，最近在纽约卡茨基尔开设的价值 40 亿美元的新大型赌场名胜世界表示，它在运营的前五个月亏损了 5800 万美元。 随着计划在附近的马萨诸塞州、宾夕法尼亚州、新泽西州和康涅狄格州开设和/或扩建新赌场，未来看起来更加黯淡。

太疯狂了。

我从没想过我会写这些话，但我们已经有足够多的赌场了。

当然，除非在得克萨斯州，那里应该有赌场。 明智的政策是关闭饱和东北部约三分之一的赌场并搬到德克萨斯州。 当然这不会发生。 无知是一种流行病——在赌场的企业文化中，在政治中，当然在懦弱的小头脑中。



原文:

I.
I was awakened by the news that Aretha Franklin had died. All respect is deserved and I will not repeat my admiration for this extraordinary woman and her extraordinary talent. There are only two artists in my life who can accept any song and their interpretation will definitely improve from the original. Ray Charles was the first. Aretha Franklin was second. Any music expert can turn the simplest song into a chorus that excites the enthusiasm of the masses. Anyone can take gospel songs, patriotic hymns, rock standards - no matter the source - and make them their own voice.

I also can't resist the temptation to stress that Aretha's death is another blow to music and a real talent that seems to dwindle as time goes on. Elton John, who has been a vocal critic of modern records like Autotune for years, tweeted this morning as he wrote: "The loss of Aretha Franklin is a blow to anyone who loves true music: music from the heart, soul and church. unique, his piano playing is underrated - he is one of my favorite pianists betting-sites.in

REST IN PEACE. soul queen

II.

I don't understand at all.

Yesterday we learned that 300 Catholic priests sexually abused more than 1,000 children. But here's the real surprise - this horrific act only happened in the state of Pennsylvania!

Country! Hundreds of religious pedophiles. thousand victims.

We can be sure that national borders have not stopped pedophilia. If these horrors had occurred in Pennsylvania, one would surmise that the predators and victims were likely to be as numerous in the 49 states and many other states. In other words, it might just be a scratch on the surface.

Something had to be done. Now. Something should have been done decades ago, but a religious institution directly responsible for more misery in the world, perhaps by another entity, remains untouched. Where is the law enforcement? Where are the morals of the church? Where is our collective anger? These buildings should be closed and sold.

Indeed, hundreds of billions of Catholic Church assets, many of which were acquired through theft, fear, and deception, must be retrieved and distributed among the victims of this horrific thought-altering institution.

All organized religion is coral and a smokescreen. It is time to confront the ignorance of the masses and our collective resistance.

I, I, I.

The casino industry is in trouble. It doesn't grow anymore. The numbers are stagnant, and demographic shifts in the years to come will only make things worse.

Casinos have made everything for themselves. corporate greed. Excessive expansion. mass saturation. parking fee. Shake retail. Unreasonable resort fees. Less success for play money. Every player knows it, every tourist knows it.

All major Las Vegas casinos saw revenue declines in the first half -- a decline despite the so-called "boom economy." Earlier this week Resorts World, a new $4 billion mega-casino that recently opened in Catskills, New York, said it lost $58 million in its first five months of operation. The future looks bleaker with plans to open and/or expand new casinos in nearby Massachusetts, Pennsylvania, New Jersey, and Connecticut.

That's crazy.

I never thought I would write these words but we already have enough casinos.

Except, of course, in Texas, where there should be a casino. The smart policy is to close about a third of the casinos in the saturated Northeast and move to Texas. Of course that won't happen. Ignorance is an epidemic - in casino corporate culture, in politics, and of course among cowardly little minds.
item.author: lisajam00747646

item.title: 高性能反应 101：`useEffect` 或 `useLayoutEffect`？ | Performant React 101: `useEffect` or `useLayoutEffect`?
item.description:
詹姆斯赢了

发表于使用 React 的前端开发人员一直使用useEffect挂钩来呈现副作用。

但是你知道有一个类似但略有不同的钩子叫做useLayoutEffect吗？ 我没有，直到我偶然发现了这个钩子的一个实例，它在我参与的一个代码库中使用。

这是一个深入了解两者之间差异的博客，并了解何时以及何时不使用它们。

区别

简而言之，区别在于精度。

  • 它是在像素的布局和绘制之后运行的异步操作。

  • 相比之下， useLayoutEffect保证立即运行。 这是一个同步操作，发生在所有 DOM 突变之后，浏览器可以绘制像素之前。 如果你熟悉 React 类组件中的componentDidMount生命周期，这基本上是等效的行为。

我们应该将它用于一切吗？

但是为什么我们不想一直使用 useLayoutEffect 呢？

没那么快。 与所有事物一样，精确是有代价的。

在使用 React 时，我们需要对副作用进行大量编程，而useEffect内置的性能增强将大大增加。

用 React 官方文档在“效果时间”下的话来说：

这使它适用于许多常见的副作用，例如设置订阅和事件处理程序，因为大多数类型的工作不应该阻止浏览器更新屏幕。

旁注：React 团队最近在 React 18 中升级useEffect以使其与用户点击等常见事件同步，更不用说你使用useLayoutEffect钩子的理由了。

那么我们什么时候应该使用useLayoutEffect呢？

这通常归结为一个主要原因：

当您需要测量 DOM 并提供计算的即时视觉反馈时（因此名称中的“布局”）。

这在这里很重要，因为不能推迟副作用。

还有一个额外的用例：

  • 正如我上面提到的， useLayoutEffect相当于 React 的类组件中旧的componentDidMount生命周期。 该钩子可用于帮助您从类组件迁移到功能组件，从而为您提供一对一的替换。 但是，这应该是一个临时修复。

外卖

将useEffect视为您可靠的丰田花冠：可靠、高性能且价格低廉 - 非常适合 99.9% 的用例。 另一方面， useLayoutEffect是一款高端法拉利：速度快、反应灵敏，但运行成本高，仅在速度真正重要时保留 0.1%。

  • 尽可能使用 useEffect 来利用 React 团队的性能优化。 仅在 (1) 您需要立即向用户直观地显示副作用时使用，或 (2) 当您临时从componentDidMount迁移时使用。



原文:

James Won

Posted on May 29

Front-end developers working with React use the useEffect hook all the time to render side-effects.  

But did you know that there is a similar but slightly different hook called useLayoutEffect? I didn't, until I stumbled on an instance of this hook used in one of the codebases I was contributing to.

This is a blog to get to the heart of the difference between the two and understand when and when not to use each.

The difference

In a nutshell the difference is about precision.

  • useEffect runs but there is no guarantee about exactly when this occurs. It is an asynchronous operation that runs after layout and painting of the pixels.

  • useLayoutEffect in contrast, is guaranteed to run immediately. It is a synchronous operation that occurs after all DOM mutations and before the browser can paint the pixels. If you are familiar with the componentDidMount lifecycle in React's class components, this is basically the equivalent behaviour.

useLayoutEffect seems superior. Should we use it for everything?

But why wouldn't we want to use useLayoutEffect all the time?

Not so fast. As with everything, precision has a cost.

React has optimised useEffect to be more performant. We need to program side-effects a lot when using React and the performance enhancements built into to useEffect will materially add up.

The React team explicitly encourages users to use useEffect as much as possible to avoid blocking visual updates.

In the words of the React official docs under 'Timing of effects':

… the function passed to useEffect fires after layout and paint, during a deferred event. This makes it suitable for the many common side effects, like setting up subscriptions and event handlers, because most types of work shouldn't block the browser from updating the screen.

Side note: The React team recently upgraded useEffect in React 18 to make it synchronous for common events such as a user clicks, even less reasons for you to use the useLayoutEffect hook.

So when should we use useLayoutEffect?

This usually boils down to one main reason:

When you need to measure the DOM and provide immediate visual feedback of the calculation (hence the 'Layout' in the name).

This is important here as side-effects cannot be deferred.

There is an additional use case: 

  • As I mentioned above, useLayoutEffect is the equivalent of the old componentDidMount lifecycle in React's class components. This hook can be used to help you migrate from class components to function components to give you a one-for-one replacement. However, this should be a temporary fix.

Takeaways

Think of useEffect as your reliable Toyota corolla: reliable, performant and cheap - perfect for 99.9% of use cases. useLayoutEffect on the other hand is a high-end Ferrari: fast, responsive, but costly to run reserved for the 0.1% when speed really matters.

  • useEffect and useLayoutEffect is both used to action side-effects, but the latter allows you more precise control when it is run.

  • Use useEffect as much as possible to take advantage of the React teams performance optimisations. There are very few genuine use cases for useLayoutEffect . Only use if (1) you need to visually show side-effects immediately to your users, or (2) when you are temporarily migrating from componentDidMount.
item.author: jwwnz

item.title: AWS 中的 Docker Swarm（设置） | Docker Swarm in AWS (set up)
item.description:
普兰贾尔-阿格拉瓦尔

发表于什么是蜂群？ 一个 swarm 由多个 Docker 主机组成，它们以所谓的 swarm 模式运行并充当管理者（管理成员关系）或工作人员（运行服务）。 给定的 Docker 主机可以是管理员、工作人员或可以同时执行这两种角色。 在集群中创建服务时，您可以定义服务的最佳状态（副本数量、服务端口、网络和存储资源等）。 管理节点：

工作节点：


工作节点也是 Docker 引擎的实例，其唯一目的是按照管理节点的指示执行容器和服务。

在 Docker 容器中托管网站并使用 Docker Swarm 管理网站的步骤。

启动 3 个 Ec2 实例，其标签为 Master、Worker-1、Worker-2、Worker-3。

使用 Xshell 连接到服务器并初始化 Docker。 命令 yum install docker* -y 启动 docker 的命令 is-systemctl start docker

启用 docker 的命令是：- systemctl enable docker

将 Docker Swarm 初始化到您的实例中。
初始化 docker Swarm 的命令：- docker swarm init

现在，将显示一个令牌。 复制此令牌并将其粘贴到其他节点中。 现在其他节点将成为 Worker 节点。

您可以在主节点中使用命令 docker node ls 检查节点的加入状态。

编写docker文件以执行以下功能。 并且 Docker 文件应该写入 VI 编辑器。

将相同的 Docker 文件复制并运行到工作节点中，因为它有助于将相同 Docker 容器的映像也构建到工作节点中。
将 docker 文件保存到 VI 编辑器并运行以下命令以将 docker 容器的映像构建到主节点中。

通过命令构建镜像 - docker image build -t appimage:1 。 您可以使用以下命令检查图像 -

泊坞窗图像 ls

现在，您必须从主节点命令 docker service create --name #name_of_the_service -p 80:80 #any_name_of_image:1 运行服务

使用命令 docker service ls 检查服务 ID。 记下此服务 ID。 使用命令创建容器：docker service scale #service_id = 4（您可以提供任意数量的容器）。 容器随机部署在任何一个 Master 或 Worker 节点上。

最后，您的 docker swarm 已配置并准备就绪。
当您点击任何工作人员或主节点的 IP 时，您将能够查看您的站点映射。

(This is the sample site I have downloaded from internet and httpd into my EC2 master instance). （这是我从 Internet 下载并 httpd 到我的 EC2 主实例中的示例站点）。



原文:

Pranjal-Agrawal

Posted on May 29

What is a Swarm? Docker Swarm is a cluster management and orchestration tool that makes it easy to scale and manage your already existing docker services.
